blocks.body: When the browser receives an HTML response, it does not simply display it. It goes through a multi-step rendering pipeline to turn raw code into pixels on your screen.

blocks.title: Parse HTML into the DOM
blocks.body: The browser reads the HTML top to bottom and builds a tree structure called the **Document Object Model (DOM)**. Each HTML tag becomes a node in the tree. When it encounters a `<link>` or `<script>` tag, it fetches those resources.

blocks.title: Parse CSS into the CSSOM
blocks.body: All CSS is parsed into a separate tree called the **CSS Object Model (CSSOM)**. This includes external stylesheets, `<style>` tags, and inline styles. The browser resolves which rules apply to which elements using CSS specificity.

blocks.title: Build the Render Tree
blocks.body: The DOM and CSSOM are combined into a **render tree** that contains only the visible elements with their computed styles. Elements with `display: none` are excluded entirely.

blocks.title: Layout (Reflow)
blocks.body: The browser calculates the exact position and size of every element in the render tree. This is called **layout** or **reflow**. Changes to element sizes or positions trigger a new layout pass.

blocks.title: Paint and Composite
blocks.body: The browser draws each layer of pixels and then composites them together into the final image you see. GPU-accelerated properties like `transform` and `opacity` can skip the layout step, making them faster to animate.

blocks.title: Performance tip
blocks.body: Changing layout properties (width, height, top, left, margin) forces the browser to recalculate layout for many elements. Prefer `transform` and `opacity` for animations -- they only trigger the paint and composite steps.
